elements: five black camera centers (points at base of each camera)
five thick red horizontal arrows pointing right, all aligned with the camera centers

interactions.label: Number of Cameras
interactions.teaches: Changing this slider shows more or fewer positions along the camera's translation path, visualizing the camera's movement as a sequence.

interactions.label: Translation Distance
interactions.teaches: This slider changes the distance each camera translates between steps, illustrating how the separation of the camera positions relates to the actual scene movement.

interactions.label: Show Motion Direction
interactions.teaches: Turning this toggle on or off shows or hides the arrows, clarifying the direction of the lateral camera translation.

demo_steps.title: Single Camera Position
demo_steps.narration: Let's start by viewing just one camera. Notice the camera is stationary—no translation is happening yet. This is our reference pose before any motion.

demo_steps.title: Visualizing Lateral Motion
demo_steps.narration: Now we've added more cameras, spaced out along a straight horizontal path. The thick red arrows indicate the direction of the lateral camera translation. This models a camera moving sideways, just like looking out the side window of a car.

demo_steps.title: Changing Distance Between Positions
demo_steps.narration: By increasing the translation distance, each camera position is now farther apart. This means the camera moves a greater distance between exposures, illustrating a faster or more pronounced lateral movement.

interactionPlan.camera_suggestion: Isometric top-front view with a gentle tilt downward, showing all camera frustums and their translation direction, so that the lateral path is obvious. Start with all cameras and arrows fully visible.
interactionPlan.notes: Ensure the arrows are thick and bright red for clarity. The camera frustums should be semi-transparent with solid black camera centers. Use equal spacing between camera positions based on 'arrowLength'.

attempts.html: ⚠ Figure failed to load

discrepancies: Rendered camera shapes are narrower and more vertical compared to the wider, squatter camera shapes in the source.
Arrows in the generated figure are shorter relative to the distance between cameras, and have much shorter/bulkier arrowheads.
There are small gaps between the arrows and the camera bodies in the generated figure, while the source connects them exactly.
The black dot at the base of each camera is relatively larger and more prominent in the source image.
The red arrows in the source are significantly longer, visually connecting the cameras tightly; in the output they're disconnected and shorter.
evaluation.notes: The interactive features and pedagogical sequence are excellent, but the proportions and alignment of cameras and arrows do not faithfully match the source.
action_items: Adjust camera symbol proportions (widen image planes, lower the vertical profile, increase size/position of black dot) to more accurately match those in the source.
Lengthen the arrows, decrease arrowhead size, and ensure they start and end exactly at the camera bases without gaps.

feedback.rationale: The issues are with the proportions, layout, and precise connection of elements, not with the conceptual plan or interaction design.
action_items: Adjust camera symbol proportions (widen image planes, lower the vertical profile, increase size/position of black dot) to more accurately match those in the source.
Lengthen the arrows, decrease arrowhead size, and ensure they start and end exactly at the camera bases without gaps.

attempts.html: ⚠ Figure failed to load

discrepancies: Camera symbols in the generated figure appear larger and bolder compared to the thinner lines in the source.
The spacing between cameras is less even and more compact than in the source image.
Arrows seem slightly shorter and thicker than those in the reference and attached more closely to camera symbols.
The number of cameras/arrows can change, but the default is 5; however, the last camera in the reference does not have an outgoing arrow, whereas the rendering always does.
The UI, narration, and slider controls are not present in the original figure (but this is expected for interactivity).
evaluation.notes: Excellent pedagogical interactivity and accurate concept, with only minor geometric/stylistic mismatches (line/arrow thickness, camera sizing) compared to the original.
action_items: Reduce the thickness of lines and arrows to better match the lighter style of the source figure.
Adjust the camera symbol dimensions to appear less bold and match the proportions in the source.
Ensure the spacing between cameras is consistent with the source and that the last camera does not show an outgoing arrow by default.
Consider adding an option to toggle UI elements on/off for a more textbook-like snapshot.
Slightly lighten or reduce the emphasis of the camera body dot for visual match.

feedback.rationale: All issues are concrete geometric and stylistic mismatches (line thickness, camera symbol size, spacing, and arrow inclusion) indicating the plan is correct but the rendering needs refinement.
action_items: Reduce the thickness of lines and arrows to better match the lighter style of the source figure.
Adjust the camera symbol dimensions to appear less bold and match the proportions in the source.
Ensure the spacing between cameras is consistent with the source and that the last camera does not show an outgoing arrow by default.
Consider adding an option to toggle UI elements on/off for a more textbook-like snapshot.
Slightly lighten or reduce the emphasis of the camera body dot for visual match.

attempts.html: ⚠ Figure failed to load

discrepancies: The generated camera symbols show extra 3D box details (extruded corners, back faces, transparent face) that are absent in the 2D source.
Arrows are not perfectly aligned with the camera centers and appear slightly offset below in the screenshot.
The sizes of the camera bodies vary less than in the source; in the source, they subtly shrink from left to right.
The black dot at the base of each camera is smaller and less visually dominant than in the source figure.
GUI overlays and text are present over the canvas in the screenshot but absent in the source figure.
evaluation.notes: The main repeated camera/arrow motif and direction are present with useful interactivity, but 3D details and UI overlays diminish faithfulness to the clean 2D original.
action_items: Remove unnecessary 3D box details and transparent faces from camera symbols—match the exact 2D linework style of the original.
Align arrow shafts precisely with the centers of the camera symbols and ensure boldness/thickness matches source.
Resize the black circle at the camera base to match the prominence of the original figure.
Hide all UI/narration overlays during screenshot/capture for pure visual fidelity.
Add slight perspective scaling or shrinking to cameras from left to right, if present in the source.
